description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone wall forming the main body, set on a round base that appears to be covered with grass and scattered stones. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof made of overlapping wooden shingles, supported by four sturdy wooden posts. A wooden crank handle is attached to one side, likely used to operate a rope or chain for drawing water, though the bucket and rope are not visible. The stonework is rough and uneven, giving the well a rustic, hand-built appearance. The roof provides shelter from debris and weather, while the open center allows access to the well's interior. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for outdoor urban or park environments.
description: A rustic stone water well with a pitched wooden shingle roof and a side-mounted wooden crank handle, set on a grassy circular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden shingle roof, and the crank handle clearly visible on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular profile of the roof and the wooden support posts, with the crank handle not visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but the crank handle is now on the right side, and the stonework and roof are clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof's triangular profile and wooden supports, with no crank handle visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden shingle roof supported by four wooden posts; no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Wooden crank handle present on the side, but no visible bucket or rope.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high surrounds the well; wooden roof provides overhead protection.
